title: Week 2 | Writing Warm-Ups
content:
Week 2 | Writing Warm-Ups

📌 Reminder: Keep your fingers moving for 5 minutes without stopping. Don’t self-edit—just write!

Day 5: The Power of Storytelling
• Concrete: Think of a moment when you had to tell a personal story to someone. How did you decide what details to include or leave out?
• Abstract: Why do people tell stories? How do narratives help shape identity and memory?
• Connecting: Compare storytelling in spoken vs. written form. How does each medium change the way a story is received?
• Exploratory: If you could tell only one story about your life to future generations, which would it be and why?

Day 6: Painting with Words (Descriptive Writing)
• Concrete: Describe a place from your childhood using all five senses—but don’t name it! Can readers guess where it is based on your details?
• Abstract: How does descriptive writing influence emotions? Can words alone create an experience as vivid as a photograph or film?
• Connecting: Think about a moment in your life that felt like a scene from a movie. How would you describe it to make it just as cinematic on paper?
context: From document: Week 2 | Writing Warm-Ups — chunk 1 (chunk 1/3)
